---

Creating a Unified Research Document for
Marketing Campaigns
This document outlines the process of creating a comprehensive research brief that consolidates product market awareness,
competitive research, and psychographic research into a single, streamlined document. This unified research document serves
as a foundation for generating various forms of marketing copy with Large Language Models (LLMs), particularly for direct
response campaigns. By organizing key research findings into one cohesive document, marketers can provide LLMs with the
necessary context to create more effective, targeted content that resonates with their audience.

-- 1 of 8 --

Purpose and Benefits of a Unified
Research Document
The unified research document serves as a central repository of essential information that can be shared with LLMs when
generating marketing assets. Rather than feeding multiple research documents into an LLM each time you need to create copy,
this consolidated approach streamlines the process and ensures consistency across all marketing materials.
Simplified Reference
Creates one comprehensive
document that contains all key
elements needed for successful
campaign implementation,
eliminating the need to
reference multiple sources.
Context for Copy
Generation
Provides LLMs with background
information and context
necessary to generate effective
direct response copy and other
marketing assets.
Focus on Relevant
Information
Excludes unnecessary details
like TAM size or specific
strategies from deep research
reports, focusing instead on
information directly relevant to
copywriting.
This approach becomes particularly valuable when generating longer-form copy, as it ensures all marketing materials maintain
consistent messaging and accurately address customer pain points, desires, and objections. The document serves as a
foundation that informs all subsequent copy generation, making the entire process more efficient and effective.

-- 2 of 8 --

Key Components of the Unified Research
Document
The unified research document follows a structured format that captures the most critical information needed for effective
marketing copy. Each component provides specific insights that help shape messaging and positioning.
Target Market
Demographic Overview
Detailed profile of the target
audience including age range,
income level, life stage, roles, digital
behavior, and shopping habits.
Focuses primarily on problem-
aware and solution-aware market
segments.
Target Market
Psychographic
Overview
In-depth analysis of customer
problems, pain points, hopes,
dreams, and ideal future state.
Explores how customers view
themselves and what
transformation they seek.
Language and
Communication Style
Specific language to use and avoid
when communicating with the
target audience, ensuring
messaging resonates appropriately.
Primary Promises and Objections
Key promises that resonate with the audience and
anticipated objections to marketing campaigns with
strategies to address them.
Existing Solutions Analysis
Overview of solutions the market has already tried and
why these solutions are inadequate, highlighting the
opportunity for your product.
Each of these components plays a crucial role in developing effective marketing copy. By understanding the target audience's
demographics, psychographics, preferred language, and objections, marketers can craft messages that truly resonate and
address specific needs and desires.

-- 3 of 8 --

Creating the Prompt for LLMs
To generate a unified research document, you'll need to provide a clear prompt to the LLM along with your research materials.
The prompt structure guides the LLM to extract and organize the most relevant information from your research documents.
"I'm working on a marketing campaign in [niche], the product is [product], and we focus on [customers] in [region]. So far, I
have performed research around product market awareness levels, competitors, and psychographic research. I'm going to
upload several PDFs that contain this research.
What I want you to do is go through these PDFs in depth and then create one unified research brief that contains all of the
key elements that would be needed in order to successfully implement this campaign. Specifically, this will be a unified
document that I'll share with you and other LLMs for background information and context as we generate additional copy
and marketing assets, most of which will be direct response oriented."
The prompt continues by specifying exactly what information you need, focusing on target market demographics,
psychographics, language preferences, promises, objections, and existing solutions. This structured approach ensures the LLM
provides a comprehensive yet focused document that will be useful for generating marketing copy.
When using this prompt, you can simply copy and paste it as is in most cases, adjusting only the specific details about your
niche, product, and target customers as needed. The prompt is designed to work effectively with various LLMs, including
Claude and ChatGPT.

-- 4 of 8 --

Case Study: Eye Serum Product
To illustrate how the unified research document works in practice, let's examine a case study for an eye serum product. This
example demonstrates the depth and structure of information that should be included in your unified document.
Target Demographics
Ages 35-60+, peak target 40-55
Primarily women, Generation X and younger baby
boomers
Middle to upper-middle income with disposable income
for premium skincare
Life stage: Peak earning years, often "sandwiched
generation" caring for children and aging parents
Role: Self-appointed "chief medical officer" of household
with sophisticated medical and nutritional literacy
Digital Behavior
Problem-aware searches: "How to get rid of dark
circles," "under-eye wrinkles," "cause of puffy eyes"
Solution-aware searches: "Best eye cream," "anti-aging
cream," "eye cream for dark circles"
Active on Facebook, increasing presence on TikTok and
Instagram for beauty content
Heavy reliance on online reviews and comparison
shopping
Values social proof over celebrity endorsements
Pain Points
Sudden appearance of wrinkles and
dark circles, skin texture changes,
looking tired and older than they feel
inside, identity crisis, loss of
confidence, societal pressure,
financial frustration from ineffective
products
Ideal Future State
Refreshed appearance, natural
radiance, smooth plump skin, bright
rested eyes, confidence in bare skin,
emotional empowerment, control
over aging, professional relevance,
personal fulfillment
Effective Promises
Visibly reduced crow's feet and fine
lines in 4-8 weeks, restored firmness
and elasticity, clinically proven
peptide technology, gentle yet
powerful formula, professional-grade
results without professional price tag
This case study demonstrates how a unified research document captures the essential information about the target audience,
their pain points, desires, and the promises that will resonate with them. This information provides a solid foundation for
creating effective marketing copy that speaks directly to the audience's needs and aspirations.

-- 5 of 8 --

Case Study: Airbnb Arbitrage Business
As a second example, let's examine a unified research document for an Airbnb arbitrage business opportunity. This case study
demonstrates how the same framework can be applied to a completely different market and product.
Effective Language
Financial freedom
Escape the rat race
Be your own boss
Take back control
Build your own empire
Smart money moves
Leverage
Cash flow
Passive income
Proven system
Language to Avoid
Get rich quick
Guru
Secret formula
Guaranteed success
Easy money
No work required
Overnight success
Overly hype-driven language
Corporate jargon
Traditional investment terminology
This case study illustrates how the unified research document framework can be adapted to different markets while
maintaining the same core structure. By understanding the specific demographics, aspirations, language preferences, and
objections of the target audience, marketers can create more effective and resonant marketing messages for any product or
service.
Demographics
Middle class individuals, primarily
employed but seeking alternatives,
U.S. based, often college-educated or
self-educated, typically 25-45 years
old with some disposable income and
credit capacity
Financial Aspirations
Desire for financial freedom, passive
income streams, ability to quit their
job, escape from corporate grind,
building wealth and legacy for their
children
Lifestyle Goals
Time freedom to travel and pursue
passions, flexibility to work from
anywhere, control over schedule,
independence from traditional
employment structures
Key Objections
Skepticism about gurus and courses,
concerns about financial risk and
hidden costs, landlord permission
issues, market saturation and
competition, time and complexity
concerns

-- 6 of 8 --

Technical Considerations and
Troubleshooting
When creating unified research documents with LLMs, you may encounter technical challenges that require troubleshooting.
Understanding these issues and having alternative approaches can help ensure successful document creation.
1 Document Formatting Issues
When uploading PDFs to LLMs, formatting issues can
sometimes cause problems. To avoid this, consider
converting documents to plain text format. For
example, when copying content from a document, use
Command-Shift-V (Mac) or Ctrl-Shift-V (Windows) to
paste as plain text without formatting, which removes
images and other elements that might cause issues.
2 LLM Limitations
Some LLMs may have limitations on the number or
size of documents they can process at once. Claude,
for instance, might occasionally show errors about
maximum image count or other limits. If you
encounter these issues, try reducing the number of
documents or their size by removing unnecessary
content.
3 Alternative Platforms
If one LLM platform is giving you trouble, consider
using alternative platforms. For example, if Claude is
experiencing issues, you might try using Poe.com
(which costs about $20/month) as it provides access
to various LLMs including Claude. This approach gives
you flexibility when one platform is experiencing
technical difficulties.
4 Timing Considerations
Sometimes LLMs like Claude might be overwhelmed
with too many inquiries, especially with Claude 4. If
you encounter errors, try again after an hour or two
when the system load might be lower. This patience
can often resolve temporary issues without requiring
alternative solutions.
By understanding these technical considerations and having backup plans, you can ensure that you're able to create unified
research documents consistently, even when facing technical challenges with specific LLM platforms.

-- 7 of 8 --

Critical Thinking and Customization
While LLMs provide excellent starting points for unified research documents, it's essential to apply critical thinking and make
customizations based on your specific knowledge of the market and product. The output from LLMs should be considered a
foundation that you can refine and enhance.
1	Review Language
Recommendations
LLMs might suggest avoiding certain language that
could actually be appropriate for your specific
product. For example, if you're selling an instant
eye lift product, terms like "instant transformation"
or "overnight results" might be accurate despite
being flagged as language to avoid. Evaluate these
recommendations in the context of your specific
product.
2 Assess Timeframe Claims
If your product delivers results faster than what
the LLM suggests (e.g., results in 2 weeks instead of
4-8 weeks), adjust these timeframes to accurately
reflect your product's performance. Be truthful
while highlighting your product's advantages.
3	Consider Emotional States
Different segments of your audience may be in
different emotional states, which affects how they
respond to messaging. What works for one
emotional state might not work for another.
Consider these nuances when reviewing language
recommendations from LLMs.
4 Adapt to Market Awareness
Depending on your product and market, you might
need to focus on different market awareness levels.
For example, GLP-1 medications might target
solution-aware people, while business
opportunities might focus on problem-aware or
even unaware segments. Adjust your document
accordingly.
Remember that while LLMs get you 95-99% of the way there, that final percentage requires your expertise and critical
thinking. The unified research document should be viewed as a living document that you can refine based on your
understanding of the market and as you gain more insights through your marketing efforts.
By combining the power of LLMs with your own expertise and critical thinking, you can create unified research documents
that serve as powerful foundations for generating effective marketing copy across various channels and formats.

-- 8 of 8 --
